openmeter/subscription/hooks/annotations.purpose: Implements a SubscriptionCommandHook (AnnotationCleanupHook) that repairs annotation-based chain links (previous/superseding subscription IDs) when a subscription is deleted, maintaining doubly-linked-list integrity across the subscription chain without orphaned pointers.

patterns.name: Embed NoOpSubscriptionCommandHook
patterns.description: Every hook struct embeds subscription.NoOpSubscriptionCommandHook so only overridden methods need implementation; the rest compile as no-ops.
patterns.example: type AnnotationCleanupHook struct { subscription.NoOpSubscriptionCommandHook; subscriptionQueryService subscription.QueryService; ... }

patterns.name: Constructor validates all deps
patterns.description: NewAnnotationCleanupHook returns (*T, error) and nil-checks every injected dependency before constructing.
patterns.example: if subscriptionQueryService == nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf("subscription query service is required") }

patterns.name: AnnotationParser for all annotation access
patterns.description: All reads/writes of annotation keys go through subscription.AnnotationParser methods — never read/write annotation map keys by string literal.

patterns.name: Clone before mutating annotations
patterns.description: Always maps.Clone the annotation map before any modification to avoid mutating the original view in place.

patterns.name: Soft-skip NotFound, hard-error otherwise
patterns.description: When fetching a linked subscription, check subscription.IsSubscriptionNotFoundError; log-and-continue on not-found, return error for all other failures.

patterns.name: Nil-out empty annotation maps
patterns.description: After deleting keys, if len(annotations)==0 set the map to nil before persisting to avoid storing empty maps.

patterns.name: Persist via SubscriptionRepository.UpdateAnnotations
patterns.description: Annotation writes use subscriptionRepo.UpdateAnnotations directly — never route through the full Service, to avoid re-triggering hooks (re-entrancy).

key_file_guides.role: Sole file: AnnotationCleanupHook with BeforeDelete repairing both superseding and previous subscription pointers on delete.
key_file_guides.watch_for: BeforeDelete aborts the delete if it errors. Both updateSupersedingSubscriptionAnnotations and updatePreviousSubscriptionAnnotations must succeed; the two helpers are symmetric — each re-points the opposite link or clears it.

anti_patterns: Reading/writing annotation keys by string literal instead of subscription.AnnotationParser.
Mutating view.Subscription.Annotations in place without maps.Clone first.
Returning an error on IsSubscriptionNotFoundError — these are expected races; log and skip.
Implementing hook methods without embedding NoOpSubscriptionCommandHook.
Storing an empty (len==0) annotations map instead of nil.

decisions.decision: Hook lives in a dedicated hooks/annotations sub-package, not inline in the service.
decisions.rationale: Isolates annotation-chain repair from core lifecycle code and avoids circular imports between service and hook.
Chain links only need repair at deletion; create/update set annotations through normal AnnotationParser flows elsewhere.

decisions.decision: Uses SubscriptionRepository directly for annotation writes, not subscription.Service.
decisions.rationale: UpdateAnnotations is a low-level repo op; routing through the full service would re-trigger hooks and risk re-entrancy.

code_examples.scenario: Wire the hook during startup in app/common
code_examples.code:
import annotationhook "github.com/openmeterio/openmeter/openmeter/subscription/hooks/annotations"

hook, err := annotationhook.NewAnnotationCleanupHook(subscriptionQuerySvc, subscriptionRepo, logger)
if err != nil { return fmt.Errorf("create annotation cleanup hook: %w", err) }
subscriptionSvc.RegisterHook(hook)
